RECAP_INTRO_HTML:
A quick note before we dive in: Pythoness Perspective now sends through Resend (from newsletter@pythonessprogrammer.com). You can choose which topics you want — not everything has to land in your inbox every week.

Use the Unsubscribe link at the bottom of this email to open your preferences. You will see four options:

  • Monthly Recap — once a month, a short personal note like this one (separate from the weekly deep dives)

  • Pythoness Perspective — weekly issues, March through November

  • Lab Notes — irregular extras when there is something worth sharing outside the main arc

  • Live Events & Reminders — save-the-date and replay notes around each live session

If weekly is too much right now, Monthly Recap alone is a valid choice. Uncheck what you do not need and hit Update — no guilt, no explanation required.

Hey there, cosmic coders.

May was an exciting moment for my homelab project. I finally have decades of past computer files on my Nextcloud. Next up: moving everything off Google Drive. I imagine that will take another year of integrating and getting off — no rush, just the next long phase.

Having a mini PC as a home server has increased my learning tenfold. I have been enjoying my own digital sovereignty with this project, and I plan to keep using it to self-host even more.

Below: everything we published in May on Mindful Automation, plus one link if you want to dig into earlier issues.
HIGHLIGHTS_HTML:
• May theme: Mindful Automation — from April’s foundation to Y.O.U.R. (Reach), error-proofing, energy patterns, and your automation playbook.

  • Hub: All five May issues tie to the Mindful Automation hub if you want the full thread in one place.

  • June: We turned to Neuroinclusive Design — readable contrast, scannable structure, and human eyes before every send.
BODY_HTML:
May on the archive

  • May 1: The foundation is built. Now let's automate on top of it.

  • May 9: If you're already talking to AI, your next move is to give it a home

  • May 18: Your energy patterns matter. So do theirs.

  • May 22: What happens when your automation breaks — and who carries that cost?

  • May 29: Your Playbook Is the System

Everything before May lives in the full newsletter archive.
